subject: Project Management
title: Kanban Enters Software
description: A quick win: understand Kanban Enters Software.

hook.text: In 2007, David Anderson applied Toyota's Kanban system to software teams. It clicked.

buildup.text: Instead of sprints, teams visualized work on a board and limited how much was in progress.

discovery.text: Kanban didn't require new roles or ceremonies — teams could start where they were.

twist.text: While Scrum demanded transformation, Kanban offered evolution. Some teams preferred the gentler path.

climax.text: Kanban proved that Agile didn't have to mean sprints — flow was an equally valid approach.

punchline.text: Not every revolution needs to be loud.

quiz.question: Who brought Kanban to software development?
